content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: File

Date: June 9, 1977

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
Nan Emmerson

Subject:

Shirley Wechsler, treasurer of Senator Roberti's committee, called June 6, 1977 with questions concerning the following situations:

Senator Roberti's committee contributed to an unsuccessful candidate in the 46th Assembly District Special Election and filed the abbreviated statement due by May 17, 1977. The committee made an additional contribution after May 14, 1977, the closing date of the second pre-election statement. Must the committee file an abbreviated post-election statement, or may it simply include the contribution in its complete off-year report which is due before the deadline for post-election statements filed re the 46th AD (August 25, 1977)?

Senator Roberti's committee contributed to an unsuccessful candidate in the 44th Assembly District Special Election subsequent to the deadline for filing second pre-election statements. Must the committee file a separate post-election statement, or may it simply include the contribution in its off-year statement which is due before the deadline for the post-election statement (September 1, 1977)?

In both instances:
- Copies of the off-year statements will be filed with the Los Angeles County, which is the largest county by population in both the 44th and 46th Assembly Districts, and
- The run-off elections are held in June.

Following a conversation with Ted, I advised Ms. Wechsler that the Roberti Committee may include the contributions in its off-year statement and need not file separate reports for the special elections.
